name: n8n Workflow Builder MCP Server
what_it_does: n8n Workflow Builder MCP server enabling AI agents to interact with n8n — the popular self-hosted workflow automation platform. Enables triggering workflows, creating and modifying workflow definitions, monitoring execution status, reading workflow outputs, and integrating n8n's vast integration ecosystem (400+ nodes) into agent-driven automation workflows.
use_cases: Triggering existing n8n workflows from orchestration agents
Creating new n8n workflow definitions from automation agents
Monitoring workflow execution status and results from DevOps agents
Building complex automation pipelines using n8n's node library from integration agents
Reading webhook data processed by n8n from data collection agents
Modifying workflow configurations dynamically from adaptive agents
not_for: Teams not using n8n (use Zapier, Make, or other automation MCPs for other platforms)
Simple single-step automations better handled directly via API
Real-time event streaming (n8n is trigger-based, not stream processing)
best_when: A team using n8n for workflow automation needs AI agents to trigger, create, and manage workflows — leveraging n8n's 400+ integrations through agent-driven automation.
avoid_when: Your automation platform is Zapier, Make, Prefect, or other non-n8n tools.

auth.notes: n8n API key required. Generate from n8n Settings → API → Create API Key. Configure N8N_BASE_URL and N8N_API_KEY environment variables. Self-hosted n8n must be running.

pricing.free_tier_limits: n8n self-hosted: free and open source; n8n Cloud: free tier with limited executions
pricing.paid_tiers: n8n Cloud paid plans; n8n Enterprise for self-hosted with support
pricing.notes: n8n is free open source for self-hosting (fair-code license). Cloud plans for managed hosting. MCP server is free.

performance.rate_limits: Self-hosted: no external limits; n8n Cloud: execution quotas per plan

known_agent_gotchas: n8n self-hosted must be running and API enabled — configure N8N_BASE_URL correctly
n8n workflow JSON format is complex — generating valid workflow definitions requires careful JSON construction
Workflow execution is asynchronous — agent must poll execution status for workflow results
n8n credentials (passwords, API keys for integrations) are separate from n8n API key — manage separately
n8n Cloud vs self-hosted API differences — verify endpoint compatibility
Workflow modifications affect production automations — test changes in staging n8n instance first
agent_readiness.security_notes: API key auth. n8n holds integration credentials for 400+ services — n8n API key grants significant access. Self-hosted: data stays on-premises. HTTPS recommended. Isolate n8n instance from production workloads for testing.

security_score_components.security_notes: API key grants access to workflow management. n8n holds integration credentials — treat API key as high privilege. HTTPS. Self-hosted option for data control.
